identity.title: Prevent semantic mockups and remediate contract proof gaps

authorization.reference: Operator required Ferric Forager to remain a native Rust rewrite/reimagination that cannot use yt-dlp as product behavior, requested every inspection finding be addressed with subagents, and then directed that project Codex prevention rules be changed first.

lifecycle.next_action: Commit the prevention rules before changing product or proof implementation, then execute the three authorized remediation lanes.

scope.summary: Close the semantic-proof loopholes exposed by the WP-004/WP-005 inspection, make the Rust-only and non-product yt-dlp boundary unambiguous, and remediate the affected compatibility, lifecycle, graph, diagnostic, and validation behavior.
scope.zero_progress_declaration: This packet is design-authorized Phase 0 prerequisite remediation. It claims zero Ferric product, capability, runtime, packaging, release, replacement-parity, or phase progress. Every changed contract and proof surface must be consumed and re-proven by a future shipped Ferric entrypoint through FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001.
in_scope: Add stable project Codex rules that prohibit product yt-dlp coupling and semantic proof substitution
Add matching build and work-packet proof obligations
Make compatibility replay execute named semantics or report a structural-only verdict without behavioral PASS wording
Require filesystem and FFmpeg effects to be acknowledged before durable or terminal lifecycle state
Replace schema-drift self-authorization with explicit compatible-transition authority
Reject relationship cycles, durable sequence zero, and undeclared diagnostic envelope fields
Add independent public-boundary counterexamples and mutation-sensitive proof
Run all canonical and independent adversarial validation
non_goals: Adding yt-dlp, Python, or a yt-dlp subprocess to shipped Ferric code
Implementing the first shipped Ferric downloader runtime slice
Claiming yt-dlp replacement parity or product runtime progress
Repeating completed project research without a named stale-source trigger
Changing final product scope or the Rust plus FFmpeg and ffprobe dependency boundary
dependencies: Existing locked Rust workspace and approved dependencies only
Validated WP-003 build gates, WP-004 compatibility assets, WP-005 contracts, and WP-012 runtime-truth gate
yt-dlp remains limited to version-pinned non-shipped compatibility-oracle generation outside Ferric product execution

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Project Codex, build rules, and packet requirements explicitly prevent product yt-dlp coupling, structural checks mislabeled as behavioral replay, self-authored proof, and unacknowledged-effect completion.

Compatibility replay executes deterministic Rust semantics for each selected case, while any offline yt-dlp oracle use remains non-shipped, version-pinned, provenance-bound build/research work and never a Ferric dependency.
Filesystem confinement and FFmpeg cancellation cannot reach durable or terminal success or release resources until every required external effect, including process reap, is acknowledged.
Diagnostic negotiation accepts drift only through an explicit compatible schema transition; durable sequence zero and undeclared envelope fields are rejected.
SourceGraph rejects prohibited relationship cycles deterministically within declared graph bounds.

Independent public-boundary tests and gate counterfactuals fail when any corrected invariant is reverted; inventory proof identifiers resolve to the behavior being proved rather than matching declarations alone.

Formatting, warnings-denied Clippy, focused and workspace tests, architecture, deep, PR, dependency-boundary, and independent adversarial checks pass while runtime truth reports the explicit prerequisite zero-progress ceiling.

checks_run: Read all required project authority, packet requirements/template, WP-004/WP-005 evidence, inspected findings, Git identity, and adversarial review skill
Verified AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are byte-identical
last_checkpoint.next_action: Commit the prevention-rule checkpoint before product remediation.

required_outcomes: Native Rust Ferric behavior with no shipped yt-dlp dependency
Semantic proof cannot be substituted by fixture integrity or declaration equality
External effects complete only after explicit acknowledgement
Compatibility drift requires independent explicit authority
risk_cases: A command labels hash/schema validation as replay PASS
A producer and consumer opt themselves into unrelated schema drift
A killed process is treated as cancelled before it is reaped
A confinement intent is treated as a durable capability before establishment
A cyclic source graph causes unbounded traversal
A zero durable sequence or undeclared wire field bypasses the contract
A validator proves only that two copies of the same claim agree

reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.candidate: no mechanism beyond the accepted outcome
reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.result: Rejected because current labels and proof maps permit structural success to be mistaken for semantic success.
Selected for existing pure contracts, lifecycle engine, compatibility corpus, xtask reports, testkit, and canonical gates.
Selected for deterministic graph traversal, checked state, and explicit allowlists.
Selected for supervised FFmpeg process intent only; yt-dlp is excluded from shipped execution.
Existing serde, serde_json, sha2, and collections are sufficient.
Selected as explicit invariants plus behavior-sensitive negative proof without new dependencies.

artifact_specific_justification.packet_requirements: Require future packets to declare semantic evidence and effect-acknowledgement invariants.
artifact_specific_justification.compatibility_tool: Make replay labels match executed behavior.
artifact_specific_justification.testkit_and_gates: Keep proof independent from the implementation declarations it judges.

change_evidence.pre_simplification_verdict: FAIL: independent compiled probes reproduced six contract violations and direct inspection proved compatibility replay emitted PASS after structural fixture validation only.
complete_post_change_verification: Parse changed JSON and YAML authority files
Verify AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md remain byte-identical
Run independent compiled public-API counterexamples and inspect generated reports

ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.current_ceiling: This packet repairs Phase 0 contract and proof prerequisites but no shipped Ferric entrypoint exists to prove product runtime behavior.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.replacement_trigger: The first shipped Rust Ferric vertical slice consumes these contracts and executes representative media work.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.likely_upgrade_path: Re-prove each consumed invariant through FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 using the exact staged Ferric artifact and actual production boundaries.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.trigger_detection_method: Product-impact classification, shipped-member inventory, runtime manifest validation, and clean staged execution fail closed.
